title: pistacia atlantica subsp. kurdica
intro: Pistacia atlantica subsp. kurdica, also known as Kurdish Pistachio or Pistachio Tree, is a subspecies of the Pistacia atlantica tree, which is native to the mountainous regions of Iran and Iraq. The bark and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues, wounds, and fever, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Pistacia atlantica subsp. kurdica include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting wound healing, making it a valuable herbal remedy. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in traditional medicine as a powder or in oil form.
